ruleset.name: Controversial Rules
ruleset.description: This ruleset contains a collection of rules that enforce C# naming conventions. The rules are adapted from phpmd's Controversial ruleset: PHP's camelCase convention becomes PascalCase for classes/methods/properties and camelCase for parameters/locals, following .NET naming guidelines.

rule.name: CamelCaseClassName
rule.message: The class {0} is not named in PascalCase.
rule.description: It is considered best practice to use PascalCase notation to name classes, structs, records, and interfaces in C#.
rule.example: class bad_name { } // violation class GoodName { } // OK

rule.name: CamelCaseMethodName
rule.message: The method {0} is not named in PascalCase.
rule.description: It is considered best practice to use PascalCase notation to name methods in C#. Constructors are excluded (their name is dictated by the class name).

rule.name: CamelCasePropertyName
rule.message: The property {0} is not named in camelCase.
rule.description: Public fields and auto-properties must be PascalCase. Private fields must be camelCase; a single leading underscore is allowed (allowUnderscorePrefix=true by default) to follow the common _camelCase convention.

property.name: allowUnderscorePrefix
property.description: Allow a single leading underscore for private fields (_camelCase).

rule.name: CamelCaseParameterName
rule.message: The parameter {0} is not named in camelCase.
rule.description: It is considered best practice to use camelCase notation to name parameters in C#.

rule.name: CamelCaseVariableName
rule.message: The variable {0} is not named in camelCase.
rule.description: It is considered best practice to use camelCase notation to name local variables in C#. A leading underscore is permitted (allowUnderscorePrefix=true by default).

property.name: allowUnderscorePrefix
property.description: Allow a single leading underscore for local variables (_camelCase).
